paper_map.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of a Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe (x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5) Be low-density steels
paper_map.abstract_summary: This work systematically investigates the influence of Be addition (0–1.5 wt%) on the density, microstructure, and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C low-density steels in both as-cast and hot-rolled (1050 °C) conditions. Be promotes a transition from single austenite to dual-phase austenite–ferrite, forms Be–Fe precipitates (needle-like in cast, spherical after rolling), and provides solid-solution strengthening and grain refinement at 0.5 wt% Be, while higher Be increases ferrite content and reduces ductility. Density is markedly reduced to ca. 6.29 g/cm³ at 1.5 wt% Be, approximately 20% lower than conventional steels, accompanied by competitive strength–ductility combinations.
paper_map.research_objective: To study the effect of beryllium (0–1.5 wt%) on the microstructural evolution and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C low-density steel and to achieve further density reduction while maintaining high specific strength.
main_process_variables: processing route: as-cast and hot rolling at 1050 °C
key_sections: Results (Microstructure and mechanical properties of as-cast states, and rolling states)
Discussion (Effect of Be on mechanical property, effect on microstructure, influence of lattice distortion and solid solution type on material density)
